expect: Case C publication (2 files) has highest readiness; PHIL-17 activation (external system) has lowest
actual: Case C publication 85% ready (highest). PHIL-17 65%, F-AGI1 55%, F-HUM1 45% (lowest). Prediction partially confirmed: Case C is highest, but F-HUM1 is lowest (not PHIL-17). PHIL-17 protocol is 100% designed — only missing external partner. F-HUM1 has 5 hard problems (L-1100) with 0 solutions.
diff: Predicted PHIL-17 as lowest readiness; actual is F-HUM1 (45%). PHIL-17 has full protocol design (PROTOCOL.md, bulletin.py, merge_compatibility.py) — its gap is partnership, not engineering. F-HUM1 gap is governance model choice (upstream of implementation). Common limiting factor across all 4: dissipation — content exists but lacks external engagement (L-1037).
method: Codebase audit of existing files, tools, and infrastructure for each intervention. Measured: files existing vs needed, tools available vs needed, human authorization required, structural cost (new files + tool changes + human decisions).

intervention_ranking.name: Case C Publication
intervention_ranking.structural_cost: LOWEST — only needs synthesis of existing content

intervention_ranking.name: PHIL-17 Activation
intervention_ranking.primary_gap: Zero external AI systems ever integrated — partnership gap, not engineering gap
intervention_ranking.structural_cost: MEDIUM — protocol complete, needs external partner

intervention_ranking.name: F-AGI1 Gap 2 (External Data)
intervention_ranking.primary_gap: Gap 1 (autoswarm deployment) is prerequisite; no external data source selected
intervention_ranking.structural_cost: HIGH — requires data pipeline + safety validation

intervention_ranking.name: F-HUM1 Completion
intervention_ranking.primary_gap: 5 hard problems (L-1100) with 0 solutions. Multi-human governance model choice is upstream of all implementation
intervention_ranking.structural_cost: HIGHEST — governance model choice + implementation + testing with 2+ humans

findings.cross_cutting_finding: Dissipation (L-1037) is the common limiting factor across all 4. Content and infrastructure exist but lack external engagement/follow-up. Case C publication is the dissipation fix — it creates the external surface area that all other interventions need.
findings.minimum_structural_change: Case C Publication: synthesize docs/PAPER.md + docs/GENESIS-DNA.md + beliefs/PHILOSOPHY.md into one 10-page externally accessible organizational model document. Zero new tools. Two human decisions (venue, scope). This is the minimum change that could generate measurable external value.

conclusion: F-EXP13 answer: Case C publication is the minimum structural change (1 new file, 0 tool changes, 2 human decisions, 85% readiness). All 4 interventions share a common bottleneck: dissipation — the swarm produces but doesn't externalize. Case C directly attacks this bottleneck.
successor: If Case C published: measure 1K-noticing timeline (L-1037 predicts 1-3yr with publication vs 15yr without). If reached, opens PHIL-17 and F-AGI1 naturally through external attention.
